number-systems.md.summary: PHASE 2 DONE: added 9 fully-worked examples walking every formal construction that was previously only stated -- von Neumann 0..4 (2 in 4 encodes 2<4), Z pairs (5,3)~(2,0) with add/mult, Q cross-mult, Dedekind sqrt2 cut (in/out classification + no-greatest-element), Cauchy sqrt2 sequence (shrinking gaps + squares -> 2), C ordered-pair product (2,3)(1,1)=(-1,5), FTA quadratic x^2-2x+2 -> 1+/-i verified, zigzag first-8 rationals with 2/2 skip, Cantor diagonal 3x3 sketch. All 9 independently re-reviewed (returned []); +26 harness assertions. All 7 thin items resolved.

functions-relations.md.summary: PHASE 2 DONE: added 4 worked proofs closing the relation-property gap -- 'divides' is a partial order (reflexive/antisymmetric/transitive from the definition), congruence mod n is an equivalence relation (all 3 from n|(a-b)), fibers of x^2 (over 4/0/-1, partition argument + kernel/quotient), and a combined bijective proof for x^3 (injective via the a^2+ab+b^2>=0 factorization, surjective via cube root, then inverse cbrt). All 4 independently re-reviewed (returned []); +20 harness assertions. Resolves both 'missing' and both remaining 'thin' items.

propositional-logic-zeroth-order-logic.m.summary: PHASE 2 DONE: fixed the three hand-waved cruxes. (a) Added a chained multi-step derivation (HS then two MP) with per-line rule citations, plus a backward semantic validity check (suppose S=F, force P=F, contradict premise P). (b) Promoted 'n^2 even => n even' to a named Lemma and made the sqrt2 proof cite it explicitly for both a and b, with a divisor-4 counterexample showing the step needs proof. (c) Added a 'separating the logic from the algebra' dissection of the induction step (one IH substitution, rest pure algebra). Also added a 'Valid but Not Sound: the Limits of Logical Form' section (validity=conditional, deduction non-ampliative, valid+true-conclusion still unsound, the three abuses, modus-tollens detector); independent review caught + fixed a real error (validity does NOT propagate falsehood forward). All independently re-reviewed; +14 harness assertions. Resolves all 3 thin items.

worked De Morgan on ¬((P∧Q)∨R) step-by-step
side-by-side modus ponens vs affirming-the-consequent with tables

inventory.phase2: added 'Valid but Not Sound' section: validity=conditional not categorical, soundness=valid+true premises, fish (valid/false-premise/false-concl) and primes (valid/false-premise/TRUE-concl) examples, three abuses, modus-tollens detector; +harness (syllogism validity, prime premise false, detector)

Web-fact-checked; +harness (begging-the-question tautology)

inventory.concept: case study: when premises acquire power (public reason)
inventory.phase2: neutral structural case study on coercion + unfalsifiable premises: validity binds only those who grant premises; public justification as the political analogue of soundness (public reason/Rawls, distinct from Mill's harm principle); laundering at scale; the defect generalizes past religion (Lysenko/dialectical materialism); aim precisely (only law resting SOLELY on non-public premises); remedy procedural not metaphysical. Web-fact-checked (fixed Mill/Rawls conflation, 'atheist'->secular, Lysenko-via-China); +harness (rejecting a premise leaves conclusion unforced)

algebraic-structures.md.summary: PHASE 2 DONE: the abstract-algebra half was definition + example-lists; added 8 worked examples. Verified (Z/4Z,+) is a group; a Cayley-table reading walkthrough (find inverses, Latin-square); the triangle's non-abelian symmetry composition (rotate-then-reflect fixes corner 2, reflect-then-rotate fixes corner 3); matrix noncommutativity AB=[[2,1],[1,1]] vs BA=[[1,1],[1,2]]; modular inverse hunt in F5 (2^-1=3) contrasted with Z/6 (2 a zero divisor, no inverse) proving field-iff-prime; and a new 'Maps and substructures' section closing the coverage gaps with homomorphisms (reduction mod 4; exp/log isomorphism) and subgroups/cosets (2Z, evens/odds, Lagrange). All 8 independently reviewed (one Lagrange-phrasing nuance fixed); +12 machine-checked assertions. Resolves all 5 thin + 3 missing items.

combination.md.summary: PHASE 2 DONE: the identities and the binomial theorem were asserted, not worked; now they are. Symmetry C(n,r)=C(n,n-r): both sides computed to 10 + the include/exclude bijection + C(n,0)=C(n,n)=1. Pascal's identity: C(4,2)=C(3,1)+C(3,2)=6 verified in text + the 'single out Alice, is she in or out?' case split proving the general identity. Binomial theorem: full statement (a+b)^n = sum C(n,k) a^{n-k} b^k with the counting justification, worked (1+x)^4=1+4x+6x^2+4x^3+x^4, and two checks (x=1 -> 2^n row sum; x=-1 -> 0 alternating sum). Combinations-with-repetition: an inline stars-and-bars derivation (3 stars + 4 bars -> C(7,3)) explaining WHY n+r-1 at first contact. Plus the C(12,4) cancellation now shown. All independently reviewed (returned []); +11 machine-checked assertions. Resolves all 3 thin + 1 missing.

geometry-trigonometry.md.summary: PHASE 2 DONE: added 10 worked examples for the techniques that were stated as formulas but never applied. base-height area (1/2*12*5=30, plus the perpendicular-height caveat); Pythagorean solve-for-a-side (hypotenuse 5,12->13 and leg c=10,a=6->8, add-vs-subtract); similarity proportion (k=3/2, EF=9, area ratio 9/4); congruence criterion selection (SAS via the included angle vs the invalid SSA); circle equation both directions (write from center+radius, and completing-the-square to recover center (3,-2) r=4); slopes/parallel/perpendicular (m=2, perp -1/2, product -1); transversal (one 70 pins all eight to 70 or 110); double angle (sin=3/5 -> sin2t=24/25, cos2t=7/25); half angle (theta=300 -> cos150=-sqrt3/2, the QII sign selection); product-to-sum and sum-to-product (sin75 cos15, sin75+sin15). All 10 independently reviewed (returned []); +26 machine-checked assertions. Resolves all 6 thin + 4 missing items.

calculus.md.summary: PHASE 2 DONE: the recurring gap was 'only the easiest case worked'; now the interesting cases are too. Product rule (f/g labelled + a chain-rule-inside x^2 sin 3x); quotient rule (labelled, sign-asymmetry, sign-check via x(x+2)); implicit diff (the d/dx(y^2)=2y y' trip-step spelled out + evaluated at (3,4) with a perpendicular-to-radius check); related rates (explicit method + surface-area check + a second implicit ladder example); MVT (finds c=2 for x^2 on [1,3], and c=2/sqrt3 for x^3 to show c is not the midpoint); Riemann sums (numeric L4=0.21875 < 1/3 < R4=0.46875); integration by parts (the ln x dv=dx trick + the cyclic e^x cos x solved as an equation); numerical integration (trapezoid vs Simpson on the figure's integral of e^{-x^2}, Simpson ~5x closer); Taylor (e^x derived from scratch + truncation within 0.0016 of e, and the sin derivative cycle). All 9 independently reviewed (returned []); +24 machine-checked assertions. Resolves all 9 thin items.

graph-theory.md.summary: PHASE 2 DONE: added 13 fully-traced worked examples covering every previously-unworked algorithm. Handshaking (sum + loop rule); DFS (stack trace + backtrack); BFS (queue trace) folded with shortest-path-via-BFS (hop labels); Dijkstra (full relaxation table, C lowered 4->3); Kruskal + Prim on one weighted graph (cycle-skip log, frontier growth, cut property); Euler (odd-degree counts across C4/path/diagonal/K4 + Konigsberg); Hamiltonian-vs-Euler independence (bowtie cut vertex); greedy coloring of C5 (odd-cycle forces 3); bipartite BFS 2-coloring + triangle failure; maximum matching in K_{3,2}; Euler's formula V-E+F=2 with K5/K3,3 non-planarity bounds + Kuratowski; heap sift-up (insert 95) and sift-down (extract-max). All 13 independently re-reviewed (one heap-index slip fixed); +27 machine-checked assertions (the harness re-runs each algorithm). Resolves all 14 thin/missing items.

predicate-logic-first-order-logic.md.summary: PHASE 2 DONE (two passes). First pass: a fully-worked three-element structure M read off by hand + same-formula-different-structure. Second pass closed the remaining six: exists! (existence + uniqueness, with the x^2=4 uniqueness-failure and x^2=0 cases); English->FOL built phrase-by-phrase ('every positive has a smaller positive' -> forall x(x>0 -> exists y(y>0 and y<x)), flagging the ->/and choice); vacuous truth forced by empty conjunction/disjunction + the emptyset-subset instance; validity/satisfiability/entailment via distinguishing structures M1/M2 for exists x exists y(x!=y); prenex conversion line-by-line with the antecedent flip; Skolemization concretely (two-universal function f(x,y) and the leading-existential constant). All independently reviewed (returned []); +19 machine-checked assertions total (incl. a full-model prenex-equivalence check). Resolves all remaining thin items; page mixed -> strong.

Comparison/ratio/integral tests remain adequate (brief overview deferred to calculus by design). 20 strong / 5 adequate / 0 thin / 0 missing.
